questions.question: What do umbrella reviews of 1,039 trials show about physical activity's effects on depression and anxiety?
options: Physical activity has no measurable effect on mental health
Physical activity shows effect sizes of −0.43 for depression and −0.42 for anxiety (moderate effects), with evidence comparable to antidepressants for mild-to-moderate depression in some populations
Only high-intensity exercise provides mental health benefits
Physical activity only works for children, not adults
questions.explanation: Robust meta-analytic evidence from umbrella reviews (97 systematic reviews, 1,039 trials) shows physical activity produces moderate effect sizes for reducing depression (−0.43) and anxiety (−0.42). A 2024 JAMA meta-analysis found exercise shows effect sizes comparable to pharmacotherapy for mild-to-moderate depression in select populations. The evidence is strong.

questions.question: How does physical PLAY differ from structured EXERCISE in terms of sustainability and motivation?
options: There is no difference — all movement is the same

Physical play (movement for enjoyment, intrinsic motivation) is more sustainable long-term than obligation-based exercise (extrinsic motivation like weight loss or 'should'), because intrinsic motivation predicts better adherence
Structured exercise is always better than playful movement
Only physical play provides mental health benefits
questions.explanation: Physical play pursued for enjoyment and intrinsic motivation ('I dance because it's joyful') is more sustainable than exercise driven by external goals ('I exercise because I should lose weight'). Both support mental health, but intrinsic motivation predicts better long-term adherence. Play-framed movement reduces the obligation burden that causes exercise dropout.

questions.question: According to the lesson, what is the micro-commitment principle for building sustainable physical play habits?
options: Commit to intense daily workouts immediately
Commit to the smallest version you're 90% confident you can do (e.g., '2 songs 3x/week' not 'workout 5 days/week'), build consistency before intensity, then scale up gradually
Never increase intensity or duration
Commitment doesn't matter for physical activity
questions.explanation: The micro-commitment principle says: commit to the smallest version you're 90% confident you'll achieve (e.g., 'dance to 2 songs 3x this week' rather than 'workout 5 days/week for an hour'). Build consistency first through ridiculously achievable commitments, then gradually scale up. This prevents the overambitious planning → failure → discouragement cycle.

questions.question: What tracking approach does the lesson recommend for sustainable physical play (versus performance-focused exercise)?
options: Track calories burned, weight loss, and performance metrics exclusively
Track enjoyment ratings (did you enjoy it? how did you feel after?) and context notes (what made it enjoyable?) rather than performance metrics, because enjoyment predicts sustainability
Never track anything
Only track if you're training for competition
questions.explanation: For sustainable physical play, track enjoyment and post-activity mood/energy (qualitative benefits) rather than performance metrics like calories, weight, speed, or distance. Enjoyment predicts long-term sustainability — if you hate an activity, you won't continue it regardless of its 'effectiveness.' Track what supports intrinsic motivation.

questions.question: Which of the following is an evidence-supported mechanism for how physical play supports mental health?
options: Physical activity has no biological effects on the brain

Movement increases endorphins and endocannabinoids (natural mood elevators), reduces cortisol (stress hormone), modulates serotonin/dopamine/norepinephrine, and interrupts rumination through behavioral activation
Only talking therapy affects neurotransmitters
Physical play only works through placebo effect
questions.explanation: Physical activity has well-documented neurochemical effects: increases endorphin and endocannabinoid release (mood elevation), reduces cortisol (stress reduction with SMD −0.37), and modulates neurotransmitters implicated in mood regulation. It also provides behavioral activation (interrupts sedentary rumination). These are biological mechanisms, not placebo.
